ffmpeg.name: ffmpeg
ffmpeg.description: A complete, cross-platform solution to record, convert and stream audio and video.
ffmpeg.docs: The main FFmpeg executable for encoding, decoding, transcoding, and streaming.

ffprobe.name: ffprobe
ffprobe.description: A simple multimedia stream analyzer. Used to gather information about media files.
ffprobe.docs: Examines media files and outputs technical metadata (codecs, streams, format, duration, etc.)

ffplay.name: ffplay
ffplay.description: A simple and portable media player using FFmpeg libraries.
ffplay.docs: Plays media files directly from the command line.

values.error: Show all errors (default for most tools). Suppresses info/warning messages.

-show_format.name: -show_format
-show_format.docs: Outputs details about the container format: duration, size, bitrate, metadata tags.

-show_streams.name: -show_streams
-show_streams.docs: Outputs details for each stream (video, audio, subtitles): codec, resolution, framerate, sample rate, etc.

-show_stream.name: -show_stream
-show_stream.docs: Same as -show_streams. Use -show_streams for the correct option name.

-show_chapters.name: -show_chapters
-show_chapters.docs: Outputs chapter markers if the container supports them.

-select_streams.name: -select_streams
-select_streams.description: Select which streams to show or process.
-select_streams.docs: Filters streams before -show_entries. Use 'v' for video, 'a' for audio, 's' for subtitles. Add :0, :1 for stream index.

-show_entries.name: -show_entries
-show_entries.description: Show only specific entries (fields) from format or streams.
-show_entries.docs: Limits output to selected keys. Format: stream=key1,key2 or format=key1. Common keys: codec_name, width, height, duration, bit_rate.

values.default: Default format. Can add options like noprint_wrappers=1:nokey=1.

formatOptions.noprint_wrappers: Remove section wrappers (e.g. [STREAM] / [/STREAM]). Use =1 to enable.
formatOptions.nokey: Output only values, not keys. Use =1 to enable. Produces compact output like 'h264' instead of 'codec_name=h264'.

-x.name: -x
-x.description: Set video display width in pixels.
-x.docs: Forces the video window to a specific width. Use with -y for fixed dimensions.

-y.name: -y
-y.description: Set video display height in pixels.
-y.docs: Forces the video window to a specific height. Use with -x for fixed dimensions.

-noborder.name: -noborder
-noborder.docs: Displays video in a borderless window for a cleaner look.

-top.name: -top
-top.description: Set window position from top of screen (in pixels).
-top.docs: Positions the video window vertically. Use 0 to align with top of screen.

-left.name: -left
-left.description: Set window position from left edge of screen (in pixels).
-left.docs: Positions the video window horizontally. Use 0 to align with left edge of screen.

-fs.name: -fs
-fs.docs: Plays video in full screen. Overrides -x and -y when active.

-an.name: -an
-an.docs: Mutes audio. Video plays with no sound. (Also used in ffmpeg to skip audio in output.)

-vn.name: -vn
-vn.docs: Hides video. Audio-only mode - useful for listening without video display. (Also used in ffmpeg to skip video in output.)

-showmode.name: -showmode
-showmode.docs: Changes how ffplay renders output. 'waves' shows audio waveform; 'rdft' shows frequency spectrum.

-loop.name: -loop
-loop.docs: Number of times to loop. 0 or -1 = infinite loop.

-i.name: -i
-i.docs: Specifies the input source. Can be a local file path or remote URL (http, https, rtsp, etc.).

-c.name: -c
-c.docs: Select encoder/decoder. Examples: -c:v libx264 (video), -c:a aac (audio), -c copy (stream copy, no re-encoding).

-codec.name: -codec
-codec.docs: Same as -c. Use :v for video, :a for audio, :s for subtitles.

-f.name: -f
-f.docs: Specifies the format (container) to use. Examples: mp4, mkv, webm, null (for discarding).

-t.name: -t
-t.docs: Stop writing after this many seconds. Can use HH:MM:SS format.

-ss.name: -ss
-ss.docs: Start reading from this timestamp. Use before -i for input seeking (fast), after for output seeking (accurate).

-to.name: -to
-to.docs: Stop writing at this timestamp (HH:MM:SS or seconds).

-vn.name: -vn
-vn.docs: No video stream in output (audio only).

-an.name: -an
-an.docs: No audio stream in output (video only).

url.docs: Network location of the media. Supports HTTP, HTTPS, RTSP, RTMP, and other protocols.

path.docs: Path to a media file on the local filesystem.
